Document:
# No metacommunications You are allowed to talk to other players outside of the game, over voice chat, text, or any other platform. This rule does not forbid communication.
What it forbids is using that communication to metagame.
## What is metagaming? Metagaming is acting on information your character could not actually know. Sharing round information with another player who is in the same round - and then acting on it - is metagaming through communication, and that is what is prohibited.
- Do not share information about the current round (who the antagonist is, where the nuke disk is, who killed who, etc.) with other players who are also in that round. - Do not act on round information that someone told you out of game.
Talking with friends is fine. Feeding each other round secrets, or coordinating in-game actions using out-of-game chat, is not.
## Examples Allowed: - Chatting in a voice call about unrelated things while you both happen to be playing. - Talking about a round after it has ended and everyone is back in the lobby.
Prohibited: - Telling a friend in a call "the traitor is the chemist, go arrest him." - Having a friend watch your screen and warn you about threats you can't see. - Coordinating an in-game attack with another player over Discord.
